clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Standardized diagnostic criteria for preeclampsia in pregnant patients after 20 weeks gestation
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Prenatal visits, triage evaluation, suspected hypertensive disorder of pregnancy
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Preeclampsia classification + severity determination + delivery/treatment routing

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for preeclampsia diagnostic evaluation
Measure SBP and DBP, confirm prior normal BP
Check 4-hour interval for mild hypertension or short interval for severe
Select proteinuria testing method (24hr urine, P/C ratio, dipstick)
Assess for thrombocytopenia, renal insufficiency, liver dysfunction, pulmonary edema, headache/visual symptoms
Display preeclampsia diagnosis + severity + next steps

validationRules: Patient must have previously normal BP
BP elevation must be confirmed on 2 occasions

rules: Check gestational age >20 weeks AND previously normal BP
Check BP criteria: (SBP ≥140 OR DBP ≥90) on 2 occasions ≥4 hours apart OR (SBP ≥160 OR DBP ≥110) confirmed within minutes
Check proteinuria criteria: ≥300 mg/24hr OR P/C ratio ≥0.3 OR dipstick 2+ (only if quantitative unavailable)
If proteinuria absent, check for new onset end-organ damage with new hypertension:
- Renal insufficiency: Creatinine >1.1 mg/dL OR doubling
- New headache unresponsive to meds (not alternative diagnosis) OR visual symptoms
If BP criteria met AND (proteinuria OR end-organ damage) → Diagnose preeclampsia
If severe hypertension (≥160/≥110) → Flag for severe features

severeFeatures.description: true if SBP ≥160 or DBP ≥110 or severe end-organ damage

recommendedNextStep.description: e.g., 'Antihypertensive therapy, delivery planning, maternal-fetal medicine referral'

thresholds.note: Severe hypertension can be confirmed within minutes (not 4 hours)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Immediate maternal-fetal medicine consult, consider delivery, IV antihypertensives, magnesium sulfate for seizure prophylaxis
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Monitor closely, initiate antihypertensive therapy, plan delivery at 37 weeks or sooner based on severity

appDisplay.screenType: High risk alert with severe feature warnings
appDisplay.button: Escalate to MFM / Emergency Delivery Protocol
appDisplay.behavior: Trigger severe preeampsia protocol, do not delay for repeat measurements

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Refer to Maternal-Fetal Medicine or Obstetric Emergency
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP every 4-6 hours, daily proteinuria and labs
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Repeat BP within minutes for severe range, otherwise 4 hours for confirmation
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Page OB provider immediately if severe features present
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log gestational age, BP readings, proteinuria method, end-organ assessment to maternal health record
